_README: Curated review decisions for suspicious appellation overlaps flagged by
The audit flags SLIVER overlaps — two appellations that are otherwise
disjoint sharing only a thin band (typically one commune thick), the
symptom of a border commune assigned to both commune lists. Some slivers
are genuine: a commune can legitimately belong to two appellations, and
two real appellations can share a small production area.
whitelist — appellation PAIRS reviewed and confirmed to overlap
legitimately. The audit reports them as ACCEPTED instead of
SUSPICIOUS. Each entry needs a public-source reason.
Pairs are unordered — ["a","b"] and ["b","a"] match the same overlap.
Use the appellation `slug` (as in wiki/_index.json / the map features).
This is a whitelist only — it does not change any geometry; fixing a real
artifact means correcting the upstream commune list / resolver, not
